query: I need help planning a feature that touches three repos: a Rails API, a React web app, and an iOS client. We have a rough product brief in Notion but no formal PRD yet, and I want a durable plan we can execute in parallel.
Can you turn this approved PRD into a TRD and break it into parallel work units? The PRD is already at .agents/spec/PRD.md and this will involve backend, web, and infra changes.
I already have a TRD for a cross-repo feature flag rollout. I need an orchestration plan with self-contained prompts for subagents and a safe way to track progress across the branch.
Please do a branch-wide audit against the TRD before we merge. The feature spans two services plus the frontend, and I want to know what's still missing versus the spec.

We finished implementation on a tall thin slice across web, API, and worker repos. I want you to analyze the branch diff, plan any missing test coverage, and only add the heavier planning docs if the test work is substantial.
I have a half-finished spec flow already: .agents/spec/TRD.md exists, but we never generated orchestration. Pick up from there and build the execution plan instead of restarting from scratch.
I'm leading a risky migration from a monolith endpoint to a new service boundary. I need a written plan, technical breakdown, and staged execution because several teams will touch different repos.
Write the final PR description for this branch, but first verify the branch is actually complete against the existing spec and test plan. If it's not complete, stop and tell me what is still open.
Help me add unit tests for src/hooks/useDebouncedValue.ts. I only changed one hook and I don't need a big plan.
Can you think through whether this new export flow belongs in the API layer or in a background job? I want architecture advice, not a PRD or workflow docs.
Plan this small ticket: add a loading spinner to the billing page and maybe tighten up the copy while you're there.
I need a PR description for a tiny bugfix branch that changes two files. No more code changes are needed.
I have a PRD already, but right now I just want help deciding between Postgres enums and a lookup table. Don't generate docs yet.
Can you review this branch and tell me what looks risky? There isn't a spec; I just want a normal code review.
Write tests for the auth middleware changes on this branch. It's one file plus one existing spec file, so let's just do the work.
I want to flesh out an idea for a startup, but only at a brainstorming level. I'm nowhere near writing PRDs or technical plans yet.
